$schema.description: Path to the JSON schema for this config file.

anyOf.description: Or type any other valid Cursor model identifier. Find the exact string in your Cursor usage dashboard (https://www.cursor.com/dashboard) — the 'Model' column there is the source of truth for identifiers Cursor's router recognizes.

model.description: Model to use for Hegel's Layer 2 LLM analysis. Use 'auto' to let Cursor pick its default fast model (usually gemini-2.5-flash). For any other choice, the identifier must match Cursor's internal routing ID exactly — display names from the model picker do NOT work (e.g. 'Grok 4.20' is the display name; 'grok-4-20-thinking' is the identifier). Unknown identifiers silently suppress Layer 2 entirely.

enableLlmAnalysis.description: Enable the LLM-powered deep analysis layer (Layer 2). When false, only fast rule-based checks (Layer 1) run. Uses tokens from your Cursor subscription.

timeoutSeconds.description: Timeout in seconds for LLM-powered hooks.

strictness.description: How aggressively Hegel intervenes. 'relaxed' = only obvious issues, 'balanced' = default, 'strict' = questions most prompts.
